mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment growth signals an impending economic slowdown, prompting institutional investors to reduce equity exposure and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within the subsequent week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 150 thousand
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in industrial production and housing starts indicates a broad-based contraction in real economic activity, forcing a repricing of cyclical equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over a 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising policy rates increase borrowing costs for highly leveraged corporations, compressing profit margins and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 60% of tightening episodes, or the macro-to-equity correlation flips sign during rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real gross domestic product expands the aggregate earnings base for public companies, mechanically supporting higher equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no statistical correlation with subsequent 5-day equity returns (correlation coefficient < 0.05), or forward returns are flat across high and low growth quartiles.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in the industrial production index below its long-term trend reflects weakening manufacturing demand, triggering profit downgrades and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The threshold breach consistently precedes positive equity returns in more than 60% of historical instances, or the predictive power vanishes after controlling for sector rotation.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising unemployment and stagnant payroll growth signals a structural labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity, forcing a downward revision in corporate revenue forecasts and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between unemployment and payroll growth shows no incremental predictive power for 60-day equity returns beyond their individual effects, or the joint signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing-led recovery regime, a sharp increase in new residential construction permits accelerates related supply chain activity and boosts consumer wealth effects, directly driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Housing starts above the threshold consistently correlate with flat or negative equity returns, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.5 over a 3-year rolling window.
